elements.sent-2411-001: We establish new Murnaghan--Nakayama rules for symplectic, orthogonal and orthosymplectic Schur functions.
elements.sent-2411-002: The classical Murnaghan--Nakayama rule expresses the product of a power sum symmetric function with a Schur function as a linear combination of Schur functions.
elements.sent-2411-003: Symplectic and orthogonal Schur functions correspond to characters of irreducible representations of symplectic and orthogonal groups.
elements.sent-2411-004: Orthosymplectic Schur functions arise as characters of orthosymplectic Lie superalgebras and are hybrids of symplectic and ordinary Schur functions.
elements.sent-2411-005: We derive explicit formulas for the product of the relevant power sum function with each of these functions, which can partly be described combinatorially using border strip manipulations.
elements.sent-2411-006: Our Murnaghan--Nakayama rules each include three distinct terms: a classical term corresponding to the addition of border strips to the relevant Young diagram, a term involving the removal of border strips, and a third term, which we describe both algebraically and combinatorially.
elements.sent-2411-007: The set of Schur functions, which are indexed by integer partitions, form a basis for the algebra of symmetric functions, so the product of two Schur functions can be expressed uniquely as a linear combination of Schur functions.
elements.sent-2411-008: The Littlewood--Richardson rule gives a combinatorial description of the coefficients that appear in the expansion.
elements.sent-2411-009: When one of the Schur functions is indexed by a one-row partition, the resulting combinatorial description is known as Pieri's rule.
elements.sent-2411-010: The power sum symmetric functions are another basis of the algebra of symmetric functions and the Murnaghan--Nakayama rule expresses the product of a Schur function with a power sum symmetric function as a linear combination of Schur functions.
elements.sent-2411-011: Given a partition $\mu$ and a positive integer $r$, \begin{equation}\label{eq:mn-rule} p_rs_{\mu}=\sum_{\lambda} (-1)^{ht(\lambda/\mu)}s_\lambda,\end{equation} where the sum is over all partitions $\lambda \supseteq \mu$, $\lambda/\mu$ is a border strip of length $r$ and $ht (\lambda/\mu)$ is one less than the number of rows in the border strip.

elements.sent-2411-037: A {\em partition $\lambda=(\lambda_1,\lambda_2,\dots)$ is a weakly decreasing sequence of nonnegative integers containing only finitely many non-zero terms.}
elements.sent-2411-038: The {\em length of a partition $\lambda$, which is the number of positive parts, is denoted by $\ell(\lambda)$.}
elements.sent-2411-039: A partition $\lambda$ can be represented pictorially as a {\em Young diagram , {in which the $i^{\text{th }$ row contains $\lambda_i$ left-justified boxes.}}}

elements.sent-2411-041: Two boxes in a skew diagram are {\em adjacent if they share a common side, and a subset of boxes in a skew diagram is {\em connected if every pair of boxes is connected through a sequence of adjacent boxes.}}
elements.sent-2411-042: A {\em border strip is a skew diagram that is connected and contains no $2 \times 2$ block of boxes.}
elements.sent-2411-043: The {\em height of a border strip $\lambda/\mu$, denoted ht($\lambda/\mu$), is one less than the number of non-empty rows in $\lambda/\mu$.}

elements.sent-2411-076: Lastly, the even orthogonal character indexed by $\lambda = (\lambda_1,\dots,\lambda_n)$ is given by \begin{equation}\label{oedef} \oe_\lambda(X) \coloneqq \frac{2 \displaystyle\det_{1\le i,j\le n}\Bigl(x_i^{\lambda_j+n-j}+\x_i^{\lambda_j+n-j}\Bigr)} {\displaystyle (1+\delta_{\lambda_n,0}) \det_{1\le i,j\le n}\Bigl(x_i^{n-j}+\bar{x}_i^{n-j}\Bigr)},\end{equation} where $\delta$ is the Kronecker delta.

elements.sent-2411-206: Orthosymplectic Schur functions, which are characters of modules for orthosymplectic Lie superalgebras, are polynomials in $2n+m$ variables $x_1^{\pm 1 ,\ldots,x_n^{\pm 1 ,y_1,\ldots,y_m.$ They can be viewed as hybrids of symplectic Schur functions and ordinary Schur functions (see \cite{benkart-1988 ).}}}

elements.sent-2411-210: For a partition $\lambda$ of length at most $n$, the polynomial \begin{equation}m_{\lambda}(X) \coloneqq \sum_{\alpha} x_1^{\alpha_1} x_2^{\alpha_2} \cdots x_n^{\alpha_n},\end{equation} where the sum is over all distinct permutations $\alpha=(\alpha_1,\dots,\alpha_n)$ of $\lambda$, is the {\em monomial symmetric function .}
elements.sent-2411-211: The {\em elementary symmetric function $e_{\lambda (X)$ indexed by a partition $\lambda$ is defined as \begin{equation}\label{def:ele} e_{\lambda}(X) \coloneqq \prod_{i=1}^n e_{\lambda_i}(X),\end{equation} where \begin{equation}e_r(X) \coloneqq \ds \sum_{1 \leq i_1 < i_2 < \dots < i_r \leq n} x_{i_1} x_{i_2} \dots x_{i_r} \text { for } r \geq 1  and  e_0(X) \coloneqq 1.\end{equation}.}}
